titleStmt.title: Letter to Eileen Corrigan from Lucy Gwynn, 11 May 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Lucy Gwynn

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter to Eileen Corrigan (d.1993) from a friend, Lucy, referring to the events of 1916. Lucy writes of the limited food supply to the University, the bravery of Eileen and the three other women who made it to Trinity on Easter Monday and of the rescheduling of the exams for those who couldn't make it on account of the military cordon.Eileen Corrigan had arrived in Dublin on 24 April 1916, the first day of the Easter Rising, from Belfast to sit her exams in the Trinity College. Eileen was one of just four female students who sat their exams as rebellion and counter attack swirled about outside Trinity’s walls. They were rewarded with lunch in Provost Mahafy’s house hosted by his wife, Frances Letitia.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
5 TRINITY COLLEGE DUBLIN. 11 May 1916. My Dear Eileen, Thank you so
much for your letter
it interested &
amused me at
the safe distance
of a past event
but I confess I
was in consternation
When I heard of the exploit
at the thought of
the risk you four
girls ran in
coming in, especially
the 2nd day â
However it will
be something to
look back on for
the rest of your
life. I am so Text is written along edge of letter. sides and top
glad you all got through.
Two men failed
hopelessly and
several others
did not come
the 2 nd day.
The exam is
repeated next Monday
for the absentees.
Miss K Price
was coming up on
Monday & got
stuck at Howth
for a week much
to the anxiety of
her people.
Miss Sandford
had her degree
conferred on the
Wednesday before Easter
she befriended me
the following week
as I was with Mrs E.
Gwynn & her 5 children
